summary: First testing run of AquaNexus MVP. Auth screens (sign-in/sign-up) render correctly with all required testIDs and empty-form validation works. However, after a successful mock sign-in, the app crashes with a RED SCREEN runtime error from @react-navigation/drawer: 'Couldn't register the navigator. Have you wrapped your app with NavigationContainer?' (thrown from useRegisterNavigator.js:15). This blocks every drawer-based flow: AI chat, Dashboard, Camera, Products, and Sign-out. No further UI testing was possible past the auth screen.

ui_bugs.issue: Red-screen runtime error: 'Couldn't register the navigator. Have you wrapped your app with NavigationContainer?' occurs on entering /(main)/ai after sign-in. Stack trace: useRegisterNavigator.js:15 -> useNavigationBuilder -> DrawerNavigator -> Drawer.Screen in (main)/_layout.tsx. The entire main app (drawer + 4 tabs + sign-out) is unreachable.

integration_issues.issue: router.replace('/(main)/ai') succeeds in navigating but the Drawer layout fails to mount due to the NavigationContainer error, leaving a blank/error screen.

react_native_issues.issue: Likely missing or mismatched @react-navigation/drawer / @react-navigation/native peer dependency, OR expo-router version that doesn't auto-provide NavigationContainer for Drawer. Verify: 1) @react-navigation/drawer is installed at a version compatible with the installed expo-router, 2) react-native-reanimated + react-native-gesture-handler are installed and app is wrapped in GestureHandlerRootView (it is in app/_layout.tsx), 3) no duplicate copies of @react-navigation/* packages in node_modules.

tests_passed: sign-in screen renders with email-input, password-input, signin-button, goto-signup-link
sign-up screen renders with name-input, email-input, password-input, signup-button, goto-signin-link
sign-in -> sign-up -> sign-in navigation via links works
Paper TextInput testIDs are correctly propagated to the underlying <input> (fill by [data-testid=...] works)
tests_blocked_by_red_screen: successful mock sign-in lands on AI Interface
drawer opens via open-drawer-button with 4 items
AI chat send -> user bubble -> typing-indicator -> AI bubble
Dashboard metric cards (temperature-card, ph-card, food-card, dashboard-tip)
Products grid, category filter chips, reorder-button confirmation
action_items: Fix the Drawer NavigationContainer crash in /app/frontend/app/(main)/_layout.tsx. Verify @react-navigation/drawer is listed in package.json and installed (yarn add @react-navigation/drawer @react-navigation/native if missing).

After install, confirm there are no duplicated copies of @react-navigation/native in node_modules (yarn why @react-navigation/native). Expo Router's Drawer must use the same instance that expo-router ships with.
If the issue persists, web_search may be required: 'expo-router Drawer Couldn't register the navigator NavigationContainer 2025 / 2026'.
Restart expo after the fix: sudo supervisorctl restart expo, then re-test the full drawer + 4 screens + sign-out flow.
critical_code_review_comments: app/(main)/_layout.tsx uses Drawer from expo-router/drawer plus DrawerContentScrollView from @react-navigation/drawer. That is the supported pattern, so the bug is almost certainly a missing/duplicate peer dependency rather than code shape.
app/index.tsx uses a 50ms setTimeout before router.replace — fine, but consider using a layout effect guarded by a 'mounted' ref for reliability.
authStore is in-memory only — reload returns to sign-in (documented behavior, matches PRD).
All interactive elements have proper testIDs. Paper TextInput propagates testID to the underlying <input> on web.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Start at https://aqua-interface-dev.preview.emergentagent.com in mobile viewport 390x844. Sign in with aquarist@test.com / password123. Paper TextInput testIDs are on the <input> itself, so use page.fill('[data-testid="email-input"]', ...) directly (do NOT append ' input'). The Drawer red-screen must be resolved before drawer-* testIDs can be reached.
rca_of_issue: Reproduction: (1) Load app URL on mobile viewport, (2) Fill email+password, (3) Click signin-button. Expected: land on AI Interface with ai-header and chat-input visible. Actual: Red screen overlay titled 'Uncaught Error — Couldn't register the navigator. Have you wrapped your app with NavigationContainer?' thrown from node_modules/@react-navigation/core/lib/module/useRegisterNavigator.js:15, with call stack useRegisterNavigator -> useNavigationBuilder -> DrawerNavigator -> callComponent.reactStackBottomFrame. RCA: expo-router's Drawer wraps children in a NavigationContainer internally, so the error strongly indicates that @react-navigation/drawer (or @react-navigation/native) resolves to a different copy than the one used by expo-router, OR @react-navigation/drawer is missing from dependencies. Mitigation: ensure @react-navigation/drawer, @react-navigation/native, react-native-reanimated, react-native-gesture-handler, react-native-screens, react-native-safe-area-context are all installed at versions aligned with the installed expo-router (see expo-router docs). Dedupe with `yarn-deduplicate` or delete node_modules + yarn.lock and reinstall if duplicates exist.
